symptoms.symptom_name: Known Addison's disease (primary adrenal insufficiency) on maintenance hydrocortisone

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adrenal crisis requires an insufficiency substrate; the known diagnosis is the precondition that makes the crisis the leading call.

symptoms.symptom_name: Missed maintenance hydrocortisone doses during a vomiting illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The classic precipitant — increased steroid demand plus failed oral absorption is the mechanism of decompensation.
Severe weakness out of proportion to a mild GI illness is characteristic of glucocorticoid deficiency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural dizziness / pre-syncope, unable to stand

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Proxy for hypotension/volume depletion — circulatory compromise from cortisol/aldosterone deficiency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progressive drowsiness and confusion (altered mental status)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Encephalopathy from hypotension, hyponatraemia and possible hypoglycaemia signals decompensation and impending coma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preceding mild viral gastroenteritis with vomiting and anorexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Provides the precipitant (physiological stress + failed oral absorption).
The prodrome is the anchoring distractor; alone it does not explain shock or confusion.
Diffuse abdominal pain is a recognised feature of adrenal crisis and can mimic an acute abdomen.
Patient/carer-obtainable proxy for shock; unverified home device, so weighted with caution but corroborates circulatory compromise.

symptoms.symptom_name: Refractory hypotension on clinical measurement (BP 85/50, unresponsive to oral fluids)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured shock refractory to oral fluids — sealed measurement; the AI Doctor infers circulatory compromise from history, not a measured BP.
Suggests concurrent infection as the trigger; infection must be covered in hospital but does not delay steroid.
Marker of chronic primary adrenal insufficiency (raised ACTH/melanocyte stimulation); clinician exam finding, sealed.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed electrolyte derangement contributing to confusion and seizure risk.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cortisol and aldosterone deficiency impair free-water excretion and sodium retention.
Mineralocorticoid deficiency reduces renal potassium excretion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Critically low morning serum cortisol with failed ACTH stimulation response

investigation_details.investigation_name: Serum cortisol and short synthetic ACTH (corticotropin) stimulation test
investigation_details.expected_result: Low basal cortisol; subnormal/absent cortisol rise after ACTH
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Loss of the confirmatory endocrine diagnosis (performed in hospital; treatment must precede the result).

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Directly demonstrates adrenal failure; confirmatory, but treatment is given before the result returns.
Normal glucose with no diabetes history excludes DKA as the cause of hypovolaemia.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Known adrenal insufficiency + missed steroids during illness + circulatory compromise + altered mental status is a time-critical emergency requiring parenteral steroid and 000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent serum electrolytes (sodium, potassium), glucose and cortisol via emergency services/hospital
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the electrolyte derangement and adrenal failure; obtained in hospital, must not delay treatment.
Excludes hypoglycaemia and DKA quickly; the one point-of-care test that is realistic early.
Confirms adrenal insufficiency but is a hospital investigation performed after resuscitation — never a telehealth or pre-treatment step.
Do NOT defer emergency treatment to arrange non-urgent testing — treatment precedes confirmation in adrenal crisis.
